description: Curated high-fidelity scenarios promoted from the cognitive trainer stack.

records.title: Engine Failure — Emergency Landing
records.briefing: You are the pilot-in-command of a single-engine Cessna 172 at 4,500 ft AGL, 7 nm east of Riverside Airport (KRAL). Suddenly the engine stops producing power. The propeller is windmilling. You have approximately 7 minutes of glide time. The ATIS last reported winds 270° at 12 kt.

cues.text: Engine has stopped. Propeller windmilling. Airspeed indicator shows 90 kts and decreasing. Altitude 4,500 ft.
Glide established. Altitude 4,100 ft. You have time for one restart attempt. Fuel selector: LEFT. Mixture: RICH. Carb heat: OFF. Magnetos: BOTH.
Engine not restarting. Altitude 3,600 ft. You can see ahead: (1) Large grass field, 1.5 nm, into wind. (2) Road — cars present. (3) Airport KRAL — 7 nm away (gl
Turning final. Altitude 600 ft AGL. Speed 70 kts. Field is clear. Wind directly on nose. Gear: fixed. You still have time for one of: full flaps, partial flaps,

emergency_steps: Immediate Actions: Pitch for best-glide speed (65 kts for C172) — Aviate first: establish best-glide speed immediately. Every second at the wrong airspeed costs altitude.

Engine Restart Attempt: Apply carb heat ON; switch fuel to BOTH; check magnetos — Carburetor ice causes ~30% of engine failures in training aircraft. Carb heat ON + fuel switching is the standard first response.

Forced Landing Field Selection: Grass field 1.5 nm — into wind, clear, within glide range — Select the closest suitable site — never stretch a glide hoping to reach a distant preferred surface. An off-airport landing on a clear field is survivable; running out of altitude short of the airport is not.

Final Approach and Landing: Full flaps — fly stabilised to touchdown — Use full flaps on the final approach to maximise drag and minimise touch-down speed, reducing kinetic energy on impact. Once committed to the field, do not change configuration.
foresight_prompts: Select the best forced-landing field using altitude/wind/surface criteria
Fly a stabilised approach to an off-airport landing
critical_thinking_prompts: Why is the expert action best at: Immediate Actions?
Why is the expert action best at: Engine Restart Attempt?
Why is the expert action best at: Forced Landing Field Selection?
debrief_rubric: Apply the 3-step immediate action drill (aviate-navigate-communicate)
Select the best forced-landing field using altitude/wind/surface criteria
Fly a stabilised approach to an off-airport landing
Mayday call content and timing

records.title: Anaphylaxis — Emergency Response
records.briefing: You are a school nurse. A 14-year-old student runs in saying they ate something with nuts by mistake. Within 2 minutes: face is swelling, hives on arms, student says throat 'feels tight'. Known nut allergy; epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) is in the medical cabinet.

cues.text: Student: swollen face, hives, throat tightness after nut ingestion. Conscious.
EpiPen given. Student improving: swelling reducing. EMS en route (8 min). At 6 min post-EpiPen, student says they feel better and wants to go to class.

records.decision_prompt: What is your immediate first action?
emergency_steps: Recognition and First Action: Administer EpiPen to outer thigh; call 999/911 — Epinephrine is the ONLY first-line treatment for anaphylaxis. Antihistamines and steroids are adjuncts — they work too slowly for airway compromise.

Post-Epinephrine Monitoring: Keep student supine; continue to monitor; await EMS — Biphasic anaphylaxis occurs in 5–20% of cases, 1–72 hours after the initial reaction. All anaphylaxis patients require hospital observation for at least 4 hours.
foresight_prompts: Recognise anaphylaxis signs rapidly (skin + airway + systemic)
Call emergency services and position patient correctly
critical_thinking_prompts: Why is the expert action best at: Recognition and First Action?
Why is the expert action best at: Post-Epinephrine Monitoring?
debrief_rubric: Recognise anaphylaxis signs rapidly (skin + airway + systemic)
Administer epinephrine as first-line treatment
Call emergency services and position patient correctly
Prepare for biphasic reaction

records.title: Active Ransomware Incident Response
records.briefing: You are the CISO. It is 2 AM. SOC alerts: 40 servers are encrypting files. Ransomware has spread from a phishing email opened 6 hours ago. Backup server status: unknown. Customer-facing services are still running. Ransom note demands $2M in 48 hours.

cues.text: 40 servers encrypting. Customer services still live. Backup status unknown.
Contained. Backup server checked: last clean backup is 18 hours old. Encryption began 6 hours ago so most critical data is in clean backup.
Recovery in progress. Board, legal, and regulator (24-hour notification requirement) must be informed. Media is asking questions on social media.

emergency_steps: Initial Containment: Isolate affected network segment; take memory snapshots; preserve logs — Containment must precede investigation. Every minute of network connectivity allows lateral spread. Take snapshots of running memory on affected hosts for forensics before shutdown.

Backup Assessment and Recovery Path: Wipe affected systems; restore from 18-hour backup; accept data loss window — An 18-hour-old clean backup is a viable recovery path. Wipe and restore is almost always faster and safer than decryption with an attacker-supplied key.

Stakeholder Communication: Board → Legal → Regulator → Customers → Public statement — Notify board and legal first (decision authority), then regulator within required window, then customers, then public statement. Never lead with public statement — it creates a vacuum you cannot control.
foresight_prompts: Containment before eradication — isolate before investigating
Preserve evidence for forensics while containing
Communication protocol: internal escalation before public statement
critical_thinking_prompts: Why is the expert action best at: Initial Containment?
Why is the expert action best at: Backup Assessment and Recovery Path?
Why is the expert action best at: Stakeholder Communication?
debrief_rubric: Containment before eradication — isolate before investigating
Preserve evidence for forensics while containing
Communication protocol: internal escalation before public statement
Never pay ransom as the first response

records.title: Engine Roughness at Cruise
records.briefing: You are flying a single-engine piston aircraft at 8,500 ft. The engine develops a slight roughness; EGT rises 50°F; fuel flow is nominal; outside air temperature is -5°C. You are 40 nm from the nearest airport.

cues.text: Engine roughness audible
EGT +50°F above normal
OAT -5°C (carburetor ice risk window)
Fuel flow nominal
40 nm to nearest airport

records.decision_prompt: Scan the situation (OODA/DECIDE): what is the correct decision and why?
foresight_prompts: What cue would warn you earliest?
critical_thinking_prompts: Ignoring OAT cue
Enriching mixture first
Not declaring precautionary early enough
debrief_rubric: Apply carburetor heat immediately; monitor EGT/RPM recovery; declare precautionary if no improvement within 60 s.
Ignoring OAT cue
Enriching mixture first
Not declaring precautionary early enough

records.title: Unresponsive Patient in Public
records.briefing: You find an adult collapsed in a shopping mall. They are unresponsive, not breathing normally. A bystander says the person 'just fell'. An AED is 30 m away. Two bystanders are present.

cues.text: Unresponsive — no response to voice/touch
Absent / agonal breathing
AED 30 m away
Two bystanders available
Bystander account: 'just fell' (not trauma)

records.decision_prompt: Scan the situation (OODA/DECIDE): what is the correct decision and why?
foresight_prompts: What cue would warn you earliest?
critical_thinking_prompts: Waiting for paramedics before starting CPR
Not delegating AED retrieval
Recovery position instead of CPR
debrief_rubric: Call emergency services → start CPR → send bystander for AED → use AED as soon as available.
Waiting for paramedics before starting CPR
Not delegating AED retrieval
Recovery position instead of CPR

records.title: Smoke in Office Building
records.briefing: You are a floor warden on the 12th floor. You smell smoke; fire alarm activates. The elevator bank shows one lift is stuck between floors. There are 25 people on your floor including one wheelchair user. The nearest stairwell door feels warm to the touch.

cues.text: Smoke smell
Fire alarm activated
Stairwell door warm — fire likely beyond
Elevator stuck — cannot use for evacuation
Wheelchair user needs assistance
25 people on floor

records.decision_prompt: Scan the situation (OODA/DECIDE): what is the correct decision and why?
foresight_prompts: What cue would warn you earliest?
critical_thinking_prompts: Opening warm stairwell door
Using elevator
Not accounting for wheelchair user
debrief_rubric: Do NOT open warm stairwell door. Use alternate stairwell. Assist wheelchair user to refuge area. Call fire brigade and report location.
Opening warm stairwell door
Using elevator
Not accounting for wheelchair user

records.title: Suspected Ransomware Alert
records.briefing: IT security alerts you at 9 AM: three workstations on the finance floor are showing unusual file-encryption activity. The payroll run is scheduled at 10 AM. Network logs show lateral movement to a file server. CEO is traveling and unreachable by phone.

cues.text: File-encryption activity on 3 workstations
Lateral movement to file server in logs
Payroll run in 60 min
CEO unreachable
Finance floor affected (payroll data at risk)

records.decision_prompt: Scan the situation (OODA/DECIDE): what is the correct decision and why?
foresight_prompts: What cue would warn you earliest?
critical_thinking_prompts: Waiting for CEO approval before isolating
Running payroll on compromised network
Not isolating file server
debrief_rubric: Isolate affected workstations and file server from network immediately; defer payroll run; escalate to CISO/deputy; invoke IR playbook.
Waiting for CEO approval before isolating
Running payroll on compromised network
Not isolating file server

records.title: Rapid drill: Engine stops on takeoff at 300 ft AGL. Runway remaining is 4
records.briefing: Engine stops on takeoff at 300 ft AGL. Runway remaining is 400 ft. Terrain ahead is flat open field. Wind is calm.

cues.text: 300 ft AGL → below decision height for turn-back

records.decision_prompt: Decide fast — choose the best option: A) Land straight ahead on remaining runway or field; B) Turn back to runway (180° turn); C) Climb and troubleshoot engine; D) Declare Mayday and wait for vectors
foresight_prompts: What is the recognition cue that short-circuits deliberation?
critical_thinking_prompts: Common failure mode under pressure: Attempting runway turn-back below 500 ft
debrief_rubric: Spot the cue: 300 ft AGL → below decision height for turn-back
Avoid the failure mode: Attempting runway turn-back below 500 ft

records.title: Rapid drill: IFR flight: autopilot disconnects, attitude indicator shows
records.briefing: IFR flight: autopilot disconnects, attitude indicator shows 60° bank. Aircraft descending at 2,000 fpm. Clouds obscure outside reference.

cues.text: High bank + descending = incipient spiral dive

records.decision_prompt: Decide fast — choose the best option: A) Level wings on attitude indicator; reduce power; pull gently; B) Pull back to stop descent first; C) Increase power to climb out; D) Declare emergency and ask for vectors
foresight_prompts: What is the recognition cue that short-circuits deliberation?
critical_thinking_prompts: Common failure mode under pressure: Pulling back before levelling wings
debrief_rubric: Spot the cue: High bank + descending = incipient spiral dive
Avoid the failure mode: Pulling back before levelling wings

records.title: Rapid drill: ER nurse: patient suddenly becomes unresponsive mid-conversa
records.briefing: ER nurse: patient suddenly becomes unresponsive mid-conversation. No pulse felt. Defibrillator is 15 ft away.

cues.text: Unresponsive + no pulse = cardiac arrest → CPR first

records.decision_prompt: Decide fast — choose the best option: A) Start CPR immediately; shout for help + AED; B) Fetch the defibrillator first (15 ft away); C) Call the code first, then start CPR; D) Recheck pulse for 10 s to confirm arrest
foresight_prompts: What is the recognition cue that short-circuits deliberation?
critical_thinking_prompts: Common failure mode under pressure: Delaying CPR to retrieve equipment
debrief_rubric: Spot the cue: Unresponsive + no pulse = cardiac arrest → CPR first
Avoid the failure mode: Delaying CPR to retrieve equipment

records.title: Rapid drill: You enter a corridor and see thick black smoke near the ceil
records.briefing: You enter a corridor and see thick black smoke near the ceiling. The fire exit is at the far end of the corridor (40 m). A stairwell is behind you (3 m).

cues.text: Black smoke near ceiling = high toxicity; closer exit available

records.decision_prompt: Decide fast — choose the best option: A) Turn back to stairwell; stay low; use exit behind you; B) Run fast through smoke to far exit; C) Shout for others then assess; D) Open window and await rescue
foresight_prompts: What is the recognition cue that short-circuits deliberation?
critical_thinking_prompts: Common failure mode under pressure: Committing to the far exit despite closer option
debrief_rubric: Spot the cue: Black smoke near ceiling = high toxicity; closer exit available
Avoid the failure mode: Committing to the far exit despite closer option

records.title: Rapid drill: SOC alert: a privileged admin account is logging in from an
records.briefing: SOC alert: a privileged admin account is logging in from an unusual country at 3 AM local time and is running a script that deletes shadow copies. The account holder is currently on holiday.

cues.text: Shadow-copy deletion = ransomware deployment in progress

records.decision_prompt: Decide fast — choose the best option: A) Disable account and isolate affected hosts immediately; B) Email the account holder to verify; C) Collect evidence for 30 min before acting; D) Alert management and await authorisation
foresight_prompts: What is the recognition cue that short-circuits deliberation?
critical_thinking_prompts: Common failure mode under pressure: Evidence collection or approval delays during active attack
debrief_rubric: Spot the cue: Shadow-copy deletion = ransomware deployment in progress
Avoid the failure mode: Evidence collection or approval delays during active attack

records.title: Rapid drill: Mass casualty event: 4 patients arrive simultaneously. A: un
records.briefing: Mass casualty event: 4 patients arrive simultaneously. A: unconscious, not breathing after repositioning airway. B: screaming, leg fracture, walking. C: silent, pale, rapid weak pulse, abdominal wound. D: minor lacerations, alert, demanding treatment.

cues.text: Silent + pale + rapid weak pulse = compensated shock → immediate

records.decision_prompt: Decide fast — choose the best option: A) Prioritise C (silent, shock signs, abdominal wound); B) Prioritise A (unconscious, not breathing); C) Prioritise B (screaming, walking); D) Prioritise D (demanding treatment)
foresight_prompts: What is the recognition cue that short-circuits deliberation?
critical_thinking_prompts: Common failure mode under pressure: Treating the loudest patient first (they are often the least critical)
debrief_rubric: Spot the cue: Silent + pale + rapid weak pulse = compensated shock → immediate
Avoid the failure mode: Treating the loudest patient first (they are often the least critical)
